Modern AI didn’t appear overnight.
It was built on three powerful algorithms that changed everything.

1️⃣ Gradient Descent
The engine of learning. It helps AI minimize errors and gradually find better solutions.

2️⃣ Backpropagation
The self-correction system. It sends errors backward through a neural network so the model can adjust and improve.

3️⃣ Transformers
The breakthrough that enabled models to understand context, relationships, and meaning in language.

Together, these ideas turned neural networks from simple pattern-matchers into systems that can write, reason, translate, and create.

From ChatGPT to Gemini and Claude, modern AI stands on the shoulders of these algorithms.

Sometimes the biggest revolutions in technology start with just a few lines of math.

Follow @artificialintelligenceeee for more insights into how AI actually works.

#ArtificialIntelligence #MachineLearning #DeepLearning #Transformers #NeuralNetworks

Blackpill (in the looksmaxxing context) is an online ideology that says a person’s life outcomes, especially dating and social respect, are mainly determined by genetics and physical appearance like facial structure and height, with self improvement having very limited impact beyond a fixed “genetic ceiling.” Followers often believe traits such as confidence, personality, fitness, or grooming do not significantly change how others perceive you if your base looks are low. In looksmaxxing spaces, blackpill ideas appear through terms like “Chad,” “mogging,” and looks ratings, often spread through dark humor memes and reels. Although looks do matter in first impressions, the blackpill view is widely criticized for being overly negative and harmful, especially for teenagers, as it can damage self esteem and ignore the real influence of mindset, health, effort, and social skills over time.
.

Here’s a fun fact: Almost every click you make online puts money in the pockets of four tech giants: Google, Amazon (AWS), Meta, and Microsoft. They’ve built the digital world’s toll booths, controlling how you search, shop, socialise, and work.

Now, they’re all heavily invested in AI, which demands massive data centres with huge environmental costs. But it’s not just about money. These companies push for weaker regulations, decide what information we access, and reshape our economies. And yeah, they’re already replacing human jobs with AI.

So, what do you say? Shall we stop giving them our money and data? Or at least minimise it?
Did you know this?👀💅🏼

Allah describes the women of Jannah with a comparison that shows unimaginable beauty and purity.

“كَأَنَّهُنَّ الْيَاقُوتُ وَالْمَرْجَانُ”
“As if they were rubies and coral.”
(Qur'an 55:58)

Rubies are among the most precious and radiant gems, glowing with deep beauty. Corals are pure, delicate, and rare. Allah uses these two treasures to describe the women of Paradise, a beauty beyond anything the human eye has ever seen.

This is a reminder that Jannah is not ordinary. Its blessings, its beauty, and its rewards are far beyond imagination.

Work for the place where Allah promises beauty like rubies, purity like coral, and happiness that will never end. 🌙

#quran #women #jannah

Your face is a reflection of your sleep.

Most guys are unknowingly ageing themselves every night — wrong sleep position, mouth breathing, cortisol spiking because they can’t get into deep sleep.

The science is clear: Growth hormone peaks during slow-wave sleep. Cortisol drops and collagen rebuilds. Lymphatic drainage clears the puffiness. Skin cell turnover doubles between 11PM–3AM.

You’re either optimising that window — or wasting it.

This carousel breaks down the exact protocol I use: supplements, timing, sleep position, circadian anchoring — all backed by peer-reviewed research.

Disclaimer: Most students did not know this happened. Neither does the admin but @dobe_ofc went to Khairpur to check up on the Mir Wah canal and then this happened accidently. 

BUT

Ah yes, Khairpur, my home town, the land of dates, darbars, dusty highways, and a nightlife so subtle it has to be detected with the Faqirs smoking up and reciting Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai and evading university sensors.

Technically, there is no “nightlife” here.
There is only after-Isha energy. I experienced it a lot as a kid. Trust me.

By 4 PM, the city is already in its evening look: half the shops are closed, the other half are pretending to close while actually just switching on Bluetooth speakers and calling it a vibe and chilling on Phatak. Somewhere near a chai dhaba, a corolla with blacked-out windows is playing Paiso Aaaaa at a volume designed to convince the universe that a rave is happening, even though the only audience is three friends who’ve always wanted to settle down in Karachi, one confused rickshaw driver, and a cat.

There are no clubs. There are no bars.
There are only parking lots with ambition.

The dance floor is wherever someone’s cousin parked his Corolla and left the doors open. The DJ is whoever remembered the Wi-Fi password. The light show is provided by a flickering street lamp and someone’s Oppo Android Phone flashlight set to “party mode.” A guy in skinny jeans catching up to 80s fashion is recording Tik Tok like he’s at Tomorrowland, even though the background is a tyre shop and a pani puri stall.

By 11 PM, Falooda that tastes like it was invented five minutes ago. The economy is being powered entirely by DC Office. 

But things are changing! There’s a change coming in and things are about to get lit and wild.

Pata nahi kyn mgr lag raha ye sahe keh rahe 😆

Don’t treat this like a miracle. Most people are carrying 1 to 3 cm of avoidable spinal compression, and once you add posture, pelvic tilt, and compensation into the mix, that can easily become another 1 to 2 cm or more.

These exercises help decompress and rehydrate the spine, improve posture, and reduce the compensations that keep you shorter than you should be. And yes, this can work at any age, because it has not really much to do with it directly, on a fundamental level, it’s mostly intradiscal manipulation driven by mechanobiology.(look up Davis’ Law and the Physical Stress Theory).

Early on, the biggest win is recompression tolerance: your discs and soft tissues get better at handling daily compression, so you stop bleeding height and start reclaiming what poor sleep, bad habits, and modern life were taking from you. But once you’ve built that hold, the decompression work starts to stick. You’re not just getting taller for an hour, you’re gradually shifting what your spine treats as normal, so remodelling can turn into adaptation and your baseline can creep up instead of snapping back.

What you’re seeing in this video is just one way to apply intradiscal manipulation. There are much better ways to do it, especially for long term spinal adaptation, because this lacks properly structured progressive overload, but I’d still say that if you’re stupid or the laziest mf in the world, this is by far your easiest and least painful way to get started, see the early results and hopefully, find enough motivation to actually get something done for once. 
 
And once you realise this stuff is real, that’s where the rest comes in. I’ve got more of the framework for free on this page and on YouTube, the full Heightmaxxing system and updates are on Skool, and for people who want a personalised plan or direct help applying it properly, DM me “true axis” for 1:1 coaching or a tailored program. All links in bio. #growtaller #heightmaxxing #heightmaxx

Becoming a gentleman is not something that happens overnight.
It is built slowly through habits, knowledge, discipline, and awareness.

Character shapes how you make decisions.
Communication shapes how people experience you.
Etiquette shapes how you behave in social situations.
Knowledge shapes how you understand the world.
And lifestyle shapes how you carry yourself every day.

None of these things require perfection. But mastering them step by step creates something rare: a man who is composed, respectful, interesting to talk to, and reliable in difficult moments.

Because in the end, being a gentleman is not about appearance.
It is about character.

🧠 Habits are not built in 21 days. They are automated patterns your brain creates to conserve energy.

⚡ The brain is wired for efficiency. New behaviors are metabolically expensive at first, so resistance is normal. Automation is the brain’s way of lowering that cost.

🔁 Habits form through a loop of cue, action, and reward. Repetition in a consistent context strengthens neural pathways and makes behavior faster and more automatic.

🌲 The more you repeat a behavior, the stronger the circuit becomes. Like carving a path in a forest, clarity comes from consistent use.

🚫 Breaking a habit is not about willpower. You do not erase old pathways, you build new ones that become more efficient through repetition and smart design.

⚠️ Big, rigid goals often fail because they overwhelm the nervous system. When energy drops, the brain defaults to familiar patterns.

📊 Elastic habits work better. Scale the behavior to your energy with Mini, Plus, and Elite options so participation stays consistent.

💪 Make the habit impossible to fail. On low energy days, do the minimum. Consistency wires the brain. Intensity is optional.

🎯 Better habits come from better neural design. Control the cue, reduce friction, lower threat, and work with your nervous system instead of against it.

#zhealth #zhealthperformance #appliedneuroscience #brainbasedtraining #movementcoach

2011 年に公開された映画 『タイタニック』 は、ジャックとローズの運命的な愛を通じ、悲劇の中でも心に深く刻まれる感情の深さを見せてくれる作品です。派手な演出よりも登場人物の感情に寄り添ったジェームズ・ キャメロン監督のスタイルは、今再視聴しても不思議と胸を打ちます。船が沈没するスペクタクルな場面よりも、二人が互いを見つめ合った静かな瞬間の方が、より強く記憶に残る映画でもあります。このエモーショナルな世界を完成させているのが、セリーヌ・ディオンの"My Heart Will Go On"です。最初は静かに始まりますが、ある瞬間から自然と胸が熱くなるような感覚を覚えます。特に後半にかけて高まっていくボーカルは、劇中のジャックとローズの最期のシーンと重なり、思わず心が締め付けられるような切なさを感じさせます。多くの人が「この曲が流れると、なぜかあの名シーンが自動的に浮かんでくる」と言いますが、それほど映画と音楽が完璧に融合しているのです。2010 年にアカデミー歌曲賞をはじめとする数々の賞を総なめにしたのは、単に人気があったからだけではありません。誰が聴いても「一つの時代の記憶」を呼び起こす力があり、時が経っても決して色あせることがないからです。 久しぶりにこの曲を聴くと、静かな夜に一人で映画を一本見終えたような、深い余韻に浸ることができます。ハッシュタグ (Hashtags) #ヴィラット・コーリ #シュブマン・ ギル #プリンス・ギル #クリケットリール #チームインディアクリケット速報 クリケットファンインド対南アフリカスポーツリールリール動画トレンドリールクリケットハイライ

Chess is one of the oldest strategy games, with its origins based on Indian civilization. Historians accept that the game emerged in the 6th century A.D. in India under the name “Chaturanga.” With its pieces reflecting military order, this game laid the foundation of modern chess.

Chaturanga consisted of pieces such as the pawn, horse, elephant, and war chariot representing the army. These pieces gradually evolved into the ancestors of modern chess pieces. In this early stage, the game was used as a training tool to teach war tactics.

As the game spread from India, it reached the Persian Empire and took the name “Shatranj.” Shatranj made the rules of chess more systematic and clarified the movement of the pieces. Thus, the second major developmental stage of chess emerged. 

Shatranj attracted great interest in the Islamic world and was accepted as an important intelligence game in intellectual circles. Thanks to strategy books written by Arab scholars, the game spread to Europe and developed even further there.

In Europe, in the 15th century, chess took a form close to its current state. The movements of the queen and bishop were strengthened, the game became faster, and modern rules took shape. This period is considered the birth of modern chess.

Today, chess has gained the status of an international sport governed by FIDE and has reached millions through digital platforms. Thanks to artificial intelligence engines, online tournaments, and educational applications, chess continues to exist as both a traditional and technological mind sport.

The pendulum wave is a classic physics demo: many pendulums with slightly different lengths are released together. They start in phase, then drift in and out of phase, and a sine wave appears to travel along the row. After one full cycle of the longest pendulum, they realign. This simulation shows exactly that, with real physics and adjustable parameters.
Each pendulum obeys simple harmonic motion. Its period is set by length and gravity: T = 2π√(L/g). So longer strings have longer periods. Here, the lengths are chosen so that in a fixed “cycle time” (e.g. 40 seconds), pendulum 1 completes 51 oscillations, pendulum 2 completes 52, and so on. After that time, they are all in phase again and the wave pattern repeats. The angle at time t is θ(t) = θ₀ cos(ωt) e^(-γt) for small angles, with ω = 2π/T. Damping (γ) makes the amplitude decay over time.
You can change pendulum count, cycle time, damping, time scale, and initial angle. Increasing the count gives a finer wave; shortening the cycle time makes the pattern repeat faster. The decorative wave in the bottom panel reflects the same harmonic motion.
Try the full interactive simulation: https://simulateitnow.com/
#PendulumWave #Physics #SimpleHarmonicMotion #ClassicalMechanics #ScienceEducation #Simulation #T2piRootLoverG #WavePhysics #Oscillations #STEM #PhysicsDemo #Education #Visualization #Interactive #Learning #Science

this video feature captures a compelling pitch on the "Meet The Drapers" stage regarding a new cancer treatment.
A female founder explains how traditional chemotherapy floods the body with toxins, causing nausea and hair loss.
She shares a personal story about her father to highlight the desperate need for a better medical solution.
Her innovation is the MediChip, a 3D-printed device designed for localized drug delivery directly to the tumor.
Instead of systemic treatment, this chip releases medicine slowly over a period of one to three months.
This method aims to eliminate severe side effects, allowing patients to continue working and living normally.
The presentation slide details the technology as a platform that avoids systemic toxicity while treating the cancer.
Judge Tim Draper engages with the concept, clarifying that the drug stays local rather than circulating through the whole body.
The pitch concludes with the powerful promise that patients can receive treatment without losing their hair or feeling sick.

The video in the clip comes from November 9, 2025, at an NFL game between the Washington Commanders and the Detroit Lions played at Northwest Stadium in Landover, Maryland. Trump was attending as part of an NFL Salute to Service halftime tribute where he participated in a military oath of enlistment ceremony for new recruits at the game. ￼

During that ceremony, he was officiating the oath of enlistment — a pledge new service members recite aloud. The traditional wording includes a prompt such as “I, state your name, do solemnly swear…” before the individual reciting the oath says their own name. ￼

The portion that went viral shows Trump reading the text on the teleprompter or script literally as “and state your name” without pausing to insert his own name, which is not how a personal oath would be read. Observers noted this, and audience audio captured boos and cheers during the moment. ￼

Many social posts framed this as a “mistake” or “Trump forgetting his own name.” Fact checkers and reporting on the incident clarified that what he was reading was simply part of the military oath wording being echoed by recruits present, not a direct self-reference. The wording is meant for the new military members to repeat their own names, not for a public figure to insert their own name. ￼

Follow @the.shared.mind for more

For centuries, belief in God has shaped human morality, purpose, and understanding of the universe.

But philosophy also raises difficult questions.

If God is all-powerful and all-loving, why does intense suffering still exist?

As science continues to explain natural mysteries, some wonder whether supernatural explanations are still necessary.

Others question why divine existence is not obvious to everyone, despite sincere belief and searching.

There is also the idea that religion may have developed to give humans comfort, meaning, and social order.

And finally, can morality exist through empathy and shared human experience - without divine command?

This post is not meant to disrespect faith. It is meant to encourage critical thinking and respectful dialogue.

What do you believe - faith, logic, or both?

.
.
.
.
.
.
.

#philosophy #deepthinking #existentialism #atheism #agnostic

Research in cognitive psychology shows that people who repeatedly watch the same shows often have very active and analytical minds.
Instead of watching only for plot, they start noticing patterns, dialogue layers, character behaviour, and small details most people miss the first time.

The brain begins to treat the show almost like a puzzle.
Lines gain new meaning.
Expressions reveal hidden emotions.
Tiny details suddenly feel intentional.

This is why highly analytical people often rewatch the same content.
They enjoy depth more than novelty.
They like exploring layers rather than consuming things quickly and moving on.

There is another psychological reason as well.
Studies show that rewatching familiar shows reduces stress and increases a feeling of control.
The brain knows what will happen next, which creates comfort and mental stability.

But here is the interesting twist.
While the story feels familiar, the mind watching it is not the same.
Each rewatch reveals something new, because your thinking evolves.

What looked simple once suddenly feels complex.
A character you disliked earlier starts making sense.
A scene that felt normal now feels brilliant.

That is why some people return to the same shows again and again.
Not because they lack options.
But because their mind enjoys depth.

Sources:
Journal of Consumer Research study on nostalgic media consumption
Journal of Experimental Social Psychology research on repeated media exposure and emotional regulation
University of Chicago research on familiarity and stress reduction

Before your heart ever beat once, thousands of cells made a silent decision — one that still shapes you today.

Long before a pulse existed,
before the heart knew how to beat,
life began in absolute silence.

Scientists have now observed the moment when cardiac cells stop acting alone — and begin behaving as one.
No heartbeat yet.
No rhythm.
Just thousands of individual cells aligning their electrical signals, synchronizing into a single coordinated wave.

That wave becomes the trigger.
The first contraction.
The foundation of a rhythm that can last an entire lifetime. 🧬⚡

Using fluorescent markers and ultra-high-resolution microscopy, researchers watched chaos turn into order in real time — as the heart built its own internal clock from nothing.

Why this matters:
🔹 Errors at this exact stage can lead to congenital heart conditions
🔹 It reshapes how we understand development, timing, and biological intelligence 🧠🔬

This isn’t just a scientific breakthrough.
It’s the origin story of every heartbeat you’ve ever had.

That first heartbeat didn’t happen by chance.
It happened because something inside sensed when to move — before movement even existed.

And somewhere along the way, most of us lost that sensitivity.
We learned to wait for proof.
For noise.
For impact.

But life never gives warnings at full volume.

____

Those who are reborn feel the shift early.
Before the system collapses.
Before the opportunity disappears.
Before the danger becomes obvious.

Rebirth exists for that reason.
Not to motivate you.
Not to hype you up.

But to re-tune the internal signal.
To bring your nervous system back to a state where it detects the shift while others are still unaware.

63 days.
To restore clarity.
To rebuild instinct.
To move while timing still belongs to you.

If something in this felt familiar —
if you know you’ve ignored that signal one too many times —
comment “ORIGIN” and I’ll send you the system.

____

#HeartScience #CellSynchronization #OriginOfLife
#FirstHeartbeat #LifeInSilence

Bill Gates has funded research into “dimming the sun.”

This is not a conspiracy theory. It is called solar geoengineering, or more precisely, solar radiation modification.

The idea? To temporarily reduce global temperatures by reflecting some of the sunlight back into space.

How? Through stratospheric aerosol injection: aircraft release reflective particles (such as sulfur) into the stratosphere to shield some of the sun’s rays.
A direct intervention on the planet’s thermostat.

Gates has funded programs such as the Harvard Solar Geoengineering Research Program, which studies the feasibility, risks, and impacts of these technologies.
For some, it is an extreme measure.
For others, it is a possible “emergency solution” if the climate reaches points of no return.

Supporters describe it as: 
– relatively inexpensive 
– technically feasible
– potentially effective in reducing heat waves and extreme weather damage

Critics call it:
– unpredictable
– risky for atmospheric balance
– a distraction from the real priority: reducing emissions

Gates himself has always emphasized that it should not replace greenhouse gas cuts, but at most complement them as a temporary measure.
And in 2025, he expressed a more nuanced position: less focused on rigid temperature targets, more oriented toward reducing human suffering.

However, the question remains:
To what extent are we willing to artificially intervene in the planet’s natural systems?
Are we trying to save the Earth... or are we entering a new phase of global climate control?

Let me know what you think in the comments and follow me @the.organic.human 

•

•

•
#matrix #climatechange #climate #biohacking

We are experiencing a fertility crisis in our society right now, but it’s not a mystery why.

Good fertility is the natural outcome of a health, balanced, nourished, detoxified system.

For women, it’s about supporting healthy ovulation and hormonal balance. 

For men, it’s about protecting sperm quality and count. 

Both come back to the same fundamentals: real food, minerals, sleep, sunlight, movement, and keeping the toxic load low.

Don’t wait until you are trying to get pregnant to start building your fertility. Healthy babies start long before conception.

If you are struggling with infertility, don’t worry. Your body will naturally regulate itself when it has the proper conditions and resources to do so.

Infertility is your body’s way of telling you something is not right.

Listen to your body’s message, take action, and watch things change before your eyes.

Jeffrey Epstein is gone, but the adrenochrome horror won’t die: how his files continue to fuel the myth of the satanic elite of QAnon.

In the wild ecosystem of modern conspiracy culture, Jeffrey Epstein—the disgraced financier accused of trafficking minors—has become a key symbol for the QAnon movement. The idea that global elites seek to extract adrenochrome from the blood of children is not only supported by pop culture but, according to QAnon experts, is also «corroborated» by Epstein’s files, flights to his private island, and his network of influential connections. Real-life evidence is intertwined with speculation and fantasy, and from this mixture of fact and fiction, a dark, viral story has grown. It is important to understand how exactly Epstein’s dossier was woven into the adrenochrome myth—and why this narrative continues to captivate millions of people despite a complete lack of scientific basis.

This video features a hilarious and awkward exchange from the British mockumentary series Cunk on Earth, where the character Philomena Cunk interviews a real-life expert about the dangers of artificial intelligence. The humor stems from Cunk’s deadpan confusion as she insists that "A, I, and U" are all vowels, leading her to demand what the "U" in AI stands for. The expert’s increasingly baffled expression and patient attempt to explain that there simply is no "U" in the acronym only fuels her conspiracy-driven logic, as she concludes that the computer must be hiding it as a secret. It’s a perfect example of her signature brand of "confident ignorance" that makes for a truly cringeworthy yet laugh-out-loud moment.

AI is turning everyone into a stock analyst.

AI tools like Claude are starting to assist with advanced financial research that once required teams of analysts.

Users can ask the model to build valuation models, review company risks, summarize earnings reports, or analyze macroeconomic trends using large financial datasets.

The system organizes data, runs calculations, and produces structured research notes that look similar to traditional analyst reports.

The catch is that the outputs still depend on the data provided, the assumptions used, and how a human interprets the results.

Financial analysis tools that were once limited to Wall Street desks are slowly becoming available to everyday investors.

Would you trust AI to help analyze your investments?

🔍 Interesting fact:

Bioarchaeology (Modern Science) — This specialized field combines archaeology and biological anthropology to study human remains, transforming old bones into detailed biographies of people who lived thousands of years ago.

By analyzing entheses—the specific points where muscles and tendons attach to the bone—archaeologists can identify “occupational stress markers.” For example, a skeletal growth known as “surfer’s ear” in ancient remains tells researchers that an individual spent significant time in cold water, while specific wear on the vertebrae and shoulders can reveal if a person was a lifelong archer, a stone-grinder, or a heavy laborer.

Beyond physical labor, scientists use stable isotope analysis on teeth to track migration. Because the chemical signature of the water you drink as a child is “locked” into your tooth enamel, an archaeologist can compare those isotopes to the local geology to prove if someone was a local or a traveler from hundreds of miles away.
Which type do you use most?

AI really said watch this 😭🤣 RAM prices feel completely disconnected from reality right now. What used to be a simple and affordable upgrade suddenly feels overpriced, leaving PC builders and gamers wondering if it is even worth it anymore. 

The surprising part is that this has very little to do with your personal computer. AI data centers are consuming massive amounts of memory, and their growing demand is putting serious pressure on global supply chains.

At the same time, chipmakers are prioritizing high margin memory like HBM for AI systems instead of standard consumer RAM. With more production going to data centers, less supply reaches everyday users and that imbalance is what is quietly driving prices higher.

Credit: @charliebcurran on X

Of course, there is no fixed age when men (or anyone) become mentally mature. Everyone grows at their own pace.

The truth is, mental performance is not just one thing. Different abilities grow at different stages of life. For example,
– Processing speed and reaction time are usually strongest in your 20s.
– Working memory often peaks in your 30s.
– Emotional control and stability keep improving in midlife.
– Vocabulary, knowledge, and life wisdom can keep growing even in your 50s and beyond.

So this does not mean the brain was immature before. It only means that experience, personality, emotional intelligence, and understanding keep developing over time.

There is no single “mental prime.” We all have different peaks in different areas throughout life. The brain keeps evolving.

Sources:
Hartshorne & Germine (2015), Psychological Science
MIT Department of Brain & Cognitive Sciences..

This image features a viral tweet centered on a scatter plot allegedly showing the correlation between testosterone levels and IQ in men. While the chart's linear fit suggests a slight negative correlation, the internet's fascination lies with the single outlier circled in red in the top-right corner. This data point represents a hypothetical individual who possesses both exceptionally high IQ (near 160) and astronomical testosterone levels (over 1600 ng/dl)—stats that are practically superhuman compared to the rest of the sample. In data science, an outlier like this might be dismissed as a measurement error or a fluke, but in the realm of internet memes, "this dude" is celebrated as the ultimate "final boss" of humanity, embodying a perfect, impossible blend of raw physical vitality and elite intelligence.
